instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `profile-block` that times a labelled body in real time, prints the elapsed seconds to *trace-output*, and returns the value of the body. The label is evaluated once.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME)))
            (LET ((#:G2 (PROGN (SORT-DATA ITEMS))))
              (FORMAT *TRACE-OUTPUT* "~&[~A] ~F seconds~%" "sort"
                      (/ (- (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME) #:G1)
                         INTERNAL-TIME-UNITS-PER-SECOND))
              #:G2))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME)))
            (LET ((#:G113 (PROGN (SORT-DATA ITEMS))))
              (FORMAT *TRACE-OUTPUT* "~&[~A] ~F seconds~%" "sort"
                      (/ (- (GET-INTERNAL-REAL-TIME) #:G112)
                         INTERNAL-TIME-UNITS-PER-SECON
